message:
bd-3uqg.7.3: OAuth bootstrap flows for GitHub Copilot and GitLab

Implement provider-specific OAuth bootstrap supporting both browser-based
authorization code flow (PKCE) and RFC 8628 device flow for CLI usage.

GitHub Copilot OAuth:
- Browser flow: start_copilot_browser_oauth() + complete_copilot_browser_oauth()
- Device flow (RFC 8628): start_copilot_device_flow() + poll_copilot_device_flow()
- DeviceCodeResponse/DeviceFlowPollResult types for structured polling
- CopilotOAuthConfig with enterprise GitHub base URL support
- parse_github_token_response() handles optional refresh_token and expiry
- Actionable diagnostic messages referencing subscription and app config

GitLab OAuth:
- Browser PKCE flow: start_gitlab_oauth() + complete_gitlab_oauth()
- GitLabOAuthConfig with self-hosted instance base URL support
- Standard OAuth token response (reuses OAuthTokenResponse)
- Diagnostic messages reference self-hosted URL in troubleshooting

Provider metadata:
- Add GitLab provider (canonical_id: "gitlab", alias: "gitlab-duo")
- Add GITHUB_TOKEN as fallback env key for github-copilot
- Add GITLAB_TOKEN + GITLAB_API_KEY env keys for gitlab

Handoff contract (bd-3uqg.7.6):
- Token refresh, rotation, cache hygiene explicitly out of scope
- Documented in code comments with integration points

Tests (31 new):
- Copilot: URL params, enterprise URLs, PKCE format, token parsing,
  missing client_id, no-refresh-token, no-expiry, diagnostics
- GitLab: URL params, self-hosted, trailing slash, no redirect_uri,
  PKCE format, token exchange, diagnostics
- Device flow: deserialization, default interval, complete URI
- Provider metadata: env key resolution for gitlab/copilot aliases
- Config defaults, trim_trailing_slash utility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -389,6 +531,28 @@ mod tests {
             provider_auth_env_keys("togetherai"),

         );
+        assert_eq!(

+        assert_eq!(provider_auth_env_keys("azure"), &["AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY"]);
+        assert_eq!(

+            &["GITHUB_COPILOT_API_KEY", "GITHUB_TOKEN"]

+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn provider_auth_env_keys_support_shared_fallbacks() {

@@ -403,4 +567,93 @@ mod tests {
     fn provider_routing_defaults_absent_for_native_adapter_only_providers() {
         assert!(provider_routing_defaults("azure-openai").is_none());
     }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn batch_a1_metadata_resolves_all_eight_providers() {
+        let ids = [
+            "302ai",
+            "abacus",
+            "aihubmix",
+            "bailing",
+            "berget",
+            "chutes",
+            "cortecs",
+            "fastrouter",
+        ];
+        for id in &ids {
+            let meta = provider_metadata(id).unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("{id} metadata missing"));
+            assert_eq!(meta.canonical_id, *id);
+            assert_eq!(
+                meta.onboarding,
+                ProviderOnboardingMode::OpenAICompatiblePreset
+            );
+        }
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn batch_a1_env_keys_match_upstream_registry() {
+        assert_eq!(provider_auth_env_keys("302ai"), &["302AI_API_KEY"]);
+        assert_eq!(provider_auth_env_keys("abacus"), &["ABACUS_API_KEY"]);
+        assert_eq!(provider_auth_env_keys("aihubmix"), &["AIHUBMIX_API_KEY"]);
+        assert_eq!(provider_auth_env_keys("bailing"), &["BAILING_API_TOKEN"]);
+        assert_eq!(provider_auth_env_keys("berget"), &["BERGET_API_KEY"]);
+        assert_eq!(provider_auth_env_keys("chutes"), &["CHUTES_API_KEY"]);
+        assert_eq!(provider_auth_env_keys("cortecs"), &["CORTECS_API_KEY"]);
+        assert_eq!(
+            provider_auth_env_keys("fastrouter"),
+            &["FASTROUTER_API_KEY"]
+        );
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn batch_a1_routing_defaults_use_openai_completions() {
+        let ids = [
+            "302ai",
+            "abacus",
+            "aihubmix",
+            "bailing",
+            "berget",
+            "chutes",
+            "cortecs",
+            "fastrouter",
+        ];
+        for id in &ids {
+            let defaults =
+                provider_routing_defaults(id).unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("{id} defaults missing"));
+            assert_eq!(defaults.api, "openai-completions", "{id} api mismatch");
+            assert!(defaults.auth_header, "{id} must use auth header");
+        }
+    }
+
+    #[test]
+    fn batch_a1_base_urls_are_distinct_and_nonempty() {
+        let ids = [
+            "302ai",
+            "abacus",
+            "aihubmix",
+            "bailing",
+            "berget",
+            "chutes",
+            "cortecs",
+            "fastrouter",
+        ];
+        let mut urls: Vec<&str> = Vec::new();
+        for id in &ids {
+            let defaults =
+                provider_routing_defaults(id).unwrap_or_else(|| panic!("{id} defaults missing"));
+            assert!(
+                !defaults.base_url.is_empty(),
+                "{id} base_url must not be empty"
+            );
+            assert!(
+                defaults.base_url.starts_with("https://"),
+                "{id} base_url must use HTTPS"
+            );
+            urls.push(defaults.base_url);
+        }
+        // All URLs must be unique.
+        urls.sort();
+        urls.dedup();
+        assert_eq!(urls.len(), ids.len(), "duplicate base URLs detected");
+    }
 }
